name: Cappellacci (Stuffed Pasta Squares)
recipeIngredient: Italian-style tomatoes: 2 cans (14.5 oz.), cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine 4 eggs, 2 tablespoons oil, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Whisk until well blended.
2. Gradually add flour, beating after each addition. Turn out onto a well-floured surface and knead several minutes until the dough is firm and smooth but not dry. Add more flour if necessary to prevent sticking. Cover and let stand at slightly warm temperature for about 30 minutes to ripen.
3. Cut the dough into 2-inch portions and roll out each portion slightly to flatten enough to fit into a pasta machine. Roll in the machine starting with the thickest setting and progressing to the next finest setting until the pasta is very thin (expansion takes place during cooking). Continue rolling until all dough is transformed into strips. Place strips on a floured surface and sprinkle lightly with flour. Cut into 4-inch squares and let rest for 10 minutes before filling and cooking.
4. Meanwhile, prepare the sauce: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until golden. Add undrained tomatoes, basil, and 2 teaspoons salt. Season with pepper to taste. Bring to a boil and simmer for 15 minutes. Add sugar at the last minute.
5. To make filling, combine ricotta, drained spinach, nutmeg, 4 eggs, and Parmesan cheese in a bowl. Mix well and season to taste with salt and pepper.
6. Bring a large pot of generously salted water to boil. Add 1 tablespoon oil. Drop each piece of pasta into the boiling water and cook for 3 to 4 minutes, or until it floats to the surface. Remove immediately and plunge into a bowl filled with ice water for 1 minute. Drain the pasta on clean cloth.
7. Place 1 tablespoon filling in the center of each pasta square. Fold the square into a triangle, then again into a smaller triangle. Press edges to seal.
8. Pour layer of tomato sauce into a large baking pan. Arrange cappellacci in rows, slightly overlapping. Cover with remaining sauce and sprinkle with additional Parmesan cheese.
9. Bake at 400°F for 5 to 7 minutes.
